title: StudioDash Security & Privacy
description: Learn how StudioDash protects your connected accounts, credentials, and developer data while keeping you in control of your information.
summary: StudioDash is designed with security and privacy at its core, helping you connect developer services confidently while protecting your data.

featureShowcases.heading: Security You Can Trust
featureShowcases.summary: StudioDash is designed to help you manage your developer services without exposing sensitive information.
featureShowcases.body: Connecting multiple developer services shouldn't mean sacrificing security. StudioDash is designed to protect credentials, clearly identify provider status, and keep sensitive information out of dashboards while giving you the operational insights you need.
bullets: Secure handling of connected provider credentials.
Clear separation between demonstration data and live information.
Provider Health helps explain connection status without exposing sensitive details.

image.alt: StudioDash provider status and security overview
image.caption: Monitor your connected services with confidence.

sections.heading: Security by Design
paragraphs: StudioDash is designed to work with developer accounts that may contain valuable business information, including analytics, revenue, advertising, website performance, and application data.

The platform follows a simple principle: request only the access required, protect sensitive information, and help developers understand the health of their connected services without exposing confidential data.
bullets: Request only the permissions required for each integration.
Display provider status without exposing sensitive values.
Keep private account information out of the user interface.

sections.heading: Connecting Your Services
paragraphs: Different developer services use different authentication methods. StudioDash supports the authentication mechanisms provided by each platform, including OAuth where available and provider-specific API credentials where required.

You're always in control of the accounts you connect and the permissions you grant. Wherever possible, StudioDash encourages the use of the minimum permissions needed for the features you want to use.
bullets: Uses provider-specific access methods where required.
Provider permissions remain under your control.

sections.heading: Local Data & Performance
paragraphs: StudioDash stores information locally to improve performance, reduce unnecessary provider requests, and make historical reporting available even after synchronisation has completed.
Cached information is treated as part of your application data and helps StudioDash deliver a faster, more responsive experience.
bullets: Provider data remains clearly separated from demonstration content.
Local application data can be cleared when required.

sections.heading: Respecting Your Privacy
paragraphs: StudioDash is designed to help you understand your own apps and services, not to publish or share your business information.
Sensitive information such as credentials, private metrics, and confidential operational data is never intended to appear in public screenshots, documentation, or demonstrations.
bullets: Clear status information without exposing sensitive details.
Public examples never include real customer data or credentials.
Provider dashboards remain the source of truth for account management.
